text: The first one is from Latvia and it's really nice, uniportal vats, sleeve resection of bronchus intermedius.
It was a carcinoid, which is obviously where we do most of our sleeves.
The next one after that is an interesting one from Turkey and this is bilateral lobectomy utilising a sub-xiphoid utility incision.
They do a lobectomy on the right, they do a lobectomy on the left.
It's quite an advanced cancer on the left.
So a whole load there for you, a little treat for the thoracic surgeons.
